facts.claim: Requests is a simple, yet elegant, HTTP library.
Requests allows you to send HTTP/1.1 requests extremely easily.
Requests is one of the most downloaded Python packages today.
Requests is depended upon by 4,000,000+ repositories.
Requests is a Python library.
Requests pulls in around 300M downloads / week.
Requests is available on PyPI.
Requests officially supports Python 3.10+.
Requests is ready for the demands of building robust and reliable HTTP–speaking applications.
Requests supports Keep-Alive & Connection Pooling.
Requests supports Sessions with Cookie Persistence.
Requests supports Browser-style TLS/SSL Verification.
Requests supports Basic & Digest Authentication.
Requests provides Automatic Content Decompression and Decoding.
Requests supports Connection Timeouts.
Requests is adding inline type annotations.
The Requests library is accessible via 'import requests'.
Requests simplifies adding query strings to URLs and form-encoding PUT & POST data.
Requests recommends using the 'json' method for PUT & POST data.
Requests supports International Domains and URLs.
Requests handles dict–like Cookies.
Requests supports Multi-part File Uploads.
Requests supports SOCKS Proxy.
Requests supports Streaming Downloads.
Requests automatically honors .netrc.
Requests supports Chunked HTTP Requests.
The REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE environment variable can override session.verify=False, causing TLS verification to still happen.
The 'requests-docs-l10n' project is published for localization of Requests documentation.
Requests fixes an issue where S3 presigned URLs with leading slashes were incorrectly modified.
Requests adds RFC 7616 support for non-Latin credentials in HTTPDigestAuth.
